after.title: Define future broker and policy boundary roadmap

context.after: The v0.1 foundation intentionally keeps broker execution and policy acceptance disabled.

question.after: What is the minimum safety design before Palari Company OS can execute broker actions or real policy authority?

recommendation.after: Keep broker and policy work documentation-only until signed gates, custody, audit, and human quorum are designed.

safe_default.after: Do not implement external side effects.

after: Fast demos are tempting but risky
Mock-only work preserves learning without side effects
Real authority requires custody and audit

after.title: Harden dry-run integration validation

acceptance_target.after: Workspace validation catches unsupported provider/action declarations, raw integration plan payload secrets, and outbox payload/source-boundary drift before any future execution boundary.

scope.after: Fail closed when hand-edited dry-run integration records declare unsupported provider actions, raw secret-looking plan payloads, or outbox payloads that differ from the approved plan.

result.after: Hardened dry-run integration validation by sharing provider/action support, scanning plan payload previews for raw secrets, and requiring outbox previews and source boundaries to match approved plans.
